title: Zero-Shot Text-to-Image Generation
summary: Introduced DALL-E, a 12-billion parameter autoregressive transformer that generates images from text descriptions. The model treats image generation as a sequence-to-sequence problem, encoding images as discrete tokens using a dVAE and generating them autoregressively conditioned on text tokens. DALL-E demonstrated remarkable ability to compose concepts, attributes, and styles it had never seen together.
key_contributions: First large-scale demonstration of text-to-image generation using a transformer
Used discrete VAE to encode images as token sequences for autoregressive generation
Showed zero-shot generalization to novel text descriptions and concept compositions
Demonstrated that language model architectures could bridge text and image modalities
impact: DALL-E demonstrated that AI could generate creative visual content from natural language descriptions, launching the text-to-image revolution. It directly inspired DALL-E 2, Stable Diffusion, and Midjourney, and triggered broad public and cultural discussion about AI creativity and copyright.
query_hooks: When could AI first generate images from text?
